questions.stem: In a unity negative feedback control system, the plant TF is 1/[s(s-1)]. Show that the system cannot be made stable with a proportional controller (gain K).
common_errors: Forgetting the unstable open-loop pole at s=+1 changes sign in CE
Applying RH without writing the correct closed-loop CE
questions.solution_method: Form the closed-loop CE: s^2 - s + K = 0. Apply RH criterion; for stability all coefficients must be positive, but the s^1 coefficient is -1 < 0 regardless of K, so no K can stabilize the system.
key_formulas: CE: s^2 + (K-1)s + ... — actually s^2 - s + K = 0 from 1 + K/[s(s-1)] = 0
RH: all first-column elements must be positive

questions.stem: For the same plant 1/[s(s-1)] in unity feedback, a derivative error controller (gain Kd) is added (PD: K + Kd*s). Is the system stable? Find the range of K and Kd for stability, and Kd for marginal stability with corresponding oscillation frequency.
common_errors: Incorrect CE formulation with PD in forward path
Missing the condition Kd > 1 as a necessary constraint
Wrong marginal stability substitution
questions.solution_method: CE with C(s)=K+Kd*s: s^2 + (Kd-1)s + K = 0. RH conditions: Kd > 1 and K > 0. For marginal stability Kd = 1; substitute s = jw into CE to find sustained oscillation frequency w = sqrt(K).

questions.stem: For plant 1/[s(s-1)] in unity feedback, design a controller combining proportional error (gain K) and output derivative (gain K1) to achieve peak overshoot 9.4% and rise time 0.221 sec. Determine K and K1.
common_errors: Confusing output derivative with error derivative in CE formulation
Incorrect extraction of zeta and wn from specs
Algebraic error when matching CE coefficients
questions.solution_method: From Mp=9.4%, find zeta=0.6; from tr=0.221s and standard formula tr=(1-0.417*zeta+0.388*zeta^2)/wn (or use pi-phi formula), find wn. Output derivative feedback modifies the effective damping without changing the steady-state gain. Write closed-loop CE, match coefficients to desired 2nd-order CE (s^2+2*zeta*wn*s+wn^2)=0 to solve for K and K1.
key_formulas: CE with output derivative: s^2 + (K1*K+1)*s ... = 0 (structure depends on block diagram)

questions.stem: For the block diagram with forward path K*(s+alpha)/[s(s+5)] and feedback 1/(s+2), show the stable region in the K-alpha plane.
common_errors: Incorrect closed-loop CE (missing feedback pole contribution)
Dropping alpha terms in RH table
Wrong boundary curve between stable/unstable regions
questions.solution_method: Form closed-loop CE from the given block diagram. Apply RH criterion treating K and alpha as parameters; derive inequalities on K and alpha. Plot boundary lines in K-alpha plane; shade stable region.
key_formulas: CE: s^3 + 7s^2 + (10 + K)s + K*alpha = 0 (verify from block diagram)
RH stability inequalities in K and alpha

questions.stem: Draw neat root contour sketches for K=11.25 and K=18.25 for the system in Q2. Show all steps.
common_errors: Not treating alpha as the varying parameter for root contours
Missing breakaway/break-in points
Incorrect asymptote angles or centroid
questions.solution_method: For fixed K, rewrite CE in standard root-locus form with alpha as the variable parameter. Apply root locus rules (poles/zeros of the new characteristic equation in alpha). Repeat for each K value and sketch both contours showing key features (starting poles, asymptotes, imaginary-axis crossings).
key_formulas: Standard RL rules: #asymptotes = n-m, centroid, angles

questions.stem: From the root locus of Q2, a dominant pole pair gives zeta=0.707 and settling time=1.5 sec (5% band). Find the corresponding K and alpha.
common_errors: Using 2% settling time formula instead of 5%
Not verifying dominance of selected pole pair
Sign errors in angle condition check
questions.solution_method: 5% settling: ts=3/(zeta*wn) => sigma=2 rad/s. With zeta=0.707, wd=sigma=2 rad/s, so dominant poles at -2+/-j2. Substitute into CE to find alpha, then K from magnitude condition.
key_formulas: Magnitude condition: K = |CE_num/CE_den| at dominant poles

questions.stem: From the given polar plot (G(jw) starts at 0.5+j0 as w->0, passes through angle -45 deg at w=2), determine G(s).
common_errors: Confusing DC gain from polar plot intercept
Wrong number of poles inferred from polar plot shape
Phase at w=2 not used to find pole/zero locations
questions.solution_method: From w->0 intercept at 0.5 (real axis), read DC gain. Shape of polar plot (semicircle-like, starting real axis) suggests first-order lag: G(s)=K/(1+Ts). Use phase condition: angle at w=2 is -45 deg => arctan(2T)=45 => T=0.5 => G(s)=0.5/(1+0.5s).

questions.stem: For the Q3 system (plant G(s) as identified, unity feedback with gain K), find K for phase margin=35 deg and the corresponding gain margin.
common_errors: Using open-loop TF without K when computing phase
Forgetting extra 1/[s(s+5)] plant in forward path
Arithmetic error in gain condition
questions.solution_method: Full open-loop: K*G(s)*(1/[s(s+5)]). Find gain crossover frequency where phase = -180+35 = -145 deg. Solve for wgc analytically or by trial. Then |OL(jwgc)| = 1 gives K. Gain margin: find phase crossover frequency (phase=-180), compute |OL| there, GM=1/|OL(jwpc)|.

questions.stem: For the Q3 system, what is the steady-state error for unit ramp input? How can this error be made zero?
common_errors: Wrong type number identification
Not accounting for the extra 1/s from plant 1/[s(s+5)]
questions.solution_method: Identify system type from OL TF. The OL has one free integrator (from 1/[s(s+5)]); type 1 system gives finite ramp error ess=1/Kv. To make ess=0 for ramp, increase to type 2 by adding an integrator (use PI or add pure integrator).

questions.stem: Draw the labeled Bode asymptotic plot for K=1 for the open-loop system in Q3.
common_errors: Incorrect slope at low frequency (should be -20 dB/dec for type-1)
Missing corner frequency from first-order G(s)
Phase plot asymptotes not labeled at correct frequencies
questions.solution_method: Write OL TF = 0.5/[(1+0.5s)*s*(s+5)/5] with K=1. Identify corner frequencies (w=2 from G, w=5 from plant denominator). Plot magnitude: starts at -20 dB/dec (type-1), breaks at each corner. Plot phase from -90 deg towards -270 deg with contributions from each pole.
key_formulas: Corner at w=1/T for each lag factor

questions.stem: For the liquid level control system (slider POT sensitivity 0.2V/cm, level sensor 0.2V/cm, KAMP=1, DC motor G1(s)=3.1/(1+0.1s) rad/s/V, valve 10 LPM/10 deg, tank G2(s)=2/(1+0.25s) cm/LPM), determine H(s)/Hin(s).
common_errors: Unit conversion error (rad/s to deg conversion for valve gain)
Missing sensitivity gains for POT and sensor in loop
Forgetting to include KAMP in forward path
questions.solution_method: Convert valve: 10 LPM / (10 deg) = 1 LPM/deg; motor output in rad/s => multiply by (180/pi) to get deg/s, then integrate? Actually angular position needed: G1 gives speed, integrate to get position (add 1/s), then apply valve sensitivity. Cascade all forward gains: Kpot * KAMP * [G1(s)/s * (10/10)*(pi/180)] * G2(s). Apply unity negative feedback with sensor gain Ks=0.2 to get closed-loop TF.

questions.stem: For the liquid level system in Q4, find the sensitivity of the closed-loop TF with respect to change in KAMP at w=1 rad/s.
common_errors: Using T sensitivity formula without partial derivative
Evaluating at wrong frequency
Forgetting KAMP appears linearly in forward path
questions.solution_method: Sensitivity S = (dT/dKAMP)*(KAMP/T) = 1/(1+L) where L is the loop gain. Since KAMP enters linearly in forward path, S_T^KAMP = 1/(1+G_OL(jw)). Evaluate |L(j1)| by substituting w=1 into open-loop expression and compute |S|.

questions.stem: For the liquid level system in Q4, draw the Nyquist plot, determine the gain margin, and comment on stability.
common_errors: Not accounting for integrator in Nyquist contour (indentation)
Incorrect phase angle calculation for OL with two time-constants
Wrong encirclement count due to polar plot shape error
questions.solution_method: Write OL TF = K_total / [s(1+0.1s)(1+0.25s)] where K_total aggregates all gains. This is type-1 with two first-order lags. Nyquist: at w=0+ starts at -90 deg (from integrator) with infinite magnitude; trace locus to w->inf. Find phase crossover (angle=-180): set phase = -90 - arctan(0.1w) - arctan(0.25w) = -180, solve for wpc. GM = 1/|OL(jwpc)|. Count encirclements of -1+j0 to assess stability.
key_formulas: wpc from tan addition formula: (0.1+0.25)wpc/[1-0.025*wpc^2]=undefined => 0.025*wpc^2=1 => wpc=sqrt(40)=6.32 rad/s

notes: 2-hour exam (not 3 hours as typical compre), MM=135. Plant in Q1 has an unstable open-loop pole at s=+1 (not s=-1). Q2 involves root contours (alpha as parameter) not standard root locus. Q3 polar plot gives a first-order G(s) to be identified. Q4 requires careful unit conversion (rad/s to LPM via motor+valve chain).
